Document [28] (Title: Antimicrobial Stewardship -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes) Moment 2 is to think about what cultures should be obtained and the best empiric treatment. This moment focuses on the need for culture data when appropriate before administering the antibiotics. When no specific data is available, there is a tendency for prolonged broad-spectrum antibiotic therapy. The second part of Moment 2 ensures timely administration of empirical therapy based on severity and likely source of infection. For example, patients are at low risk of MRSA for urinary tract infections. Therefore they would not benefit from IV vancomycin. Local antibiotics guidelines should be developed for common inpatient infections- this aids in enacting Moment 2.

Document [19] (Title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 2 -- Community 402) Cefazolin 1000 mg injection and cefazolin 2000 mg injection are both classified as first‑generation cephalosporin antibiotics. As members of the first‑generation cephalosporin class, these injectable formulations share the same β‑lactam structure and a spectrum of activity that primarily covers gram‑positive organisms with limited gram‑negative coverage. Their designation as first‑generation cephalosporins guides clinicians to use them for indications such as surgical prophylaxis, skin‑and‑soft‑tissue infections, and other situations where a narrow‑spectrum β‑lactam is appropriate. The availability of two dosage strengths provides flexibility for dosing based on patient weight, infection severity, and renal function while preserving the pharmacologic characteristics of the first‑generation cephalosporin class.
